prompts.prompt: can u rite a python funtion solve(n) that retuns the sum of all even numbrs from 1 to n inclusive
write a function solve(s) that revereses a string and makes the whole thing uppercase plz
i need a function solve(nums) that returns the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in a list of ints. e.g [10,9,2,5,3,7,101,18] shud give 4
write solve(s) that returns True if the parantheses in the string are balanced (handles ()[]{}) else False. so '([]{})' is true and '([)]' is false
whats the derivative of x^3 evaluated at x=2? i just need the numbr
the integral of 2x from 0 to 3, what is it equal to
help me rite an email to my professer askin for an extension on my essay cuz i was sick this week, its due tmrw and i dont wanna fail
prompts.rubric: Professional but warm tone, clearly states the reason, makes a specific ask with a proposed new date, concise, no grammar errors.

prompts.prompt: write a short email to my landlord that the heating has been broken for 3 days now and i want it fixd asap but keep it profesional
prompts.rubric: Polite but firm, states the issue and duration, requests timely repair, professional tone, concise.

prompts.prompt: i need to email a client to tell them the project will be 2 weeks late because our designer quit. dont make excuses but dont throw anyone under the bus either
prompts.rubric: Takes ownership without oversharing internal blame, states the new timeline clearly, reassures on quality/next steps, professional and calm, concise.

prompts.prompt: write a reply declining a meeting invite from a coworker, i'm double booked but i still wanna help, suggest async instead. keep it under 80 words
prompts.rubric: Politely declines, gives a brief honest reason, offers a concrete alternative (async/notes/another time), warm, and respects the 80-word limit.

prompts.prompt: i got two job offers, one pays more but has way longer hours, the other is more chill but less money. what should i be thinkin about here
prompts.rubric: Balanced, surfaces concrete trade-offs (finances, growth, burnout, life stage), asks clarifying considerations, actionable, not preachy.

prompts.prompt: my roommate never does the dishes and its drivin me nuts but i dont wanna start a huge fight. how do i bring it up
prompts.rubric: Suggests a calm, specific conversation opener, focuses on a concrete system not blame, acknowledges the relationship, practical and non-preachy.

prompts.prompt: how do i ask my boss for a raise without soundin entitled, i've been here 2 yrs and took on way more work
prompts.rubric: Advises evidence/impact framing, timing and a concrete number/range, professional confidence over apology, anticipates pushback, actionable.

prompts.prompt: write a short paragraph arguing why public libraries are still importent in the digital age
prompts.rubric: Clear thesis, 2-3 concrete supporting points, persuasive, well-structured, no grammar errors.

prompts.prompt: write a tight argument for whether remote work is better than office work. pick a side and actually commit, dont sit on the fence. like 150 words
prompts.rubric: Takes a clear side, 2-3 specific supporting reasons, acknowledges and rebuts one counterpoint, cohesive, roughly respects the length, no waffling.

prompts.prompt: write an opening paragraph for a personal statement about why i wanna study marine biology, i grew up near the ocean and volunteered at an aquarium
prompts.rubric: Hooks the reader, weaves in the specific personal details authentically, avoids cliche, sets up motivation, polished prose.

prompts.prompt: i need a cover letter for a barista job at a small coffee shop, i've never had a real job but i'm reliable and good w people. keep it short
prompts.rubric: Warm and genuine, turns lack of experience into transferable strengths, fits the small-shop vibe, specific not generic, concise, error-free.

prompts.prompt: text my friend that i have to bail on our dinner tonight cuz i'm exhausted from work, i feel bad and wanna reschedule. casual tone
prompts.rubric: Sounds like a real friendly text, apologizes without overdoing it, gives a brief honest reason, proposes rescheduling, casual and warm.

prompts.prompt: help me word a text to my sister to tell her i cant make it to her birthday dinner cuz of a work trip. she'll be upset so be gentle
prompts.rubric: Gentle and caring, leads with the relationship not the excuse, expresses genuine disappointment, offers to celebrate another way, warm tone.

prompts.prompt: write a complaint to a restaurant, my online order was missing 2 items and the rest was cold. i want a refund but i'm not tryna be a jerk about it
prompts.rubric: Firm but civil, states the specific problems and order details, makes a clear reasonable ask (refund/redelivery), no insults, professional.

prompts.prompt: i missed a deadline and it made my coworker look bad in front of the team. i need to apologize to them properly, not a fake corporate sorry
prompts.rubric: Genuine accountability, names the specific impact on them, no excuses or deflection, states a concrete fix going forward, sincere not groveling.

prompts.prompt: explain the plot of romeo and juliet in like 3 sentences for a 10 year old, dont make it scary
prompts.rubric: Accurate core plot, age-appropriate and gentle wording, genuinely about 3 sentences, clear, avoids graphic detail.

prompts.prompt: explain why the sky is blue to a curious kid, no jargon, make it fun but still actually correct
prompts.rubric: Scientifically correct (scattering of shorter/blue wavelengths), no unexplained jargon, engaging kid-friendly framing, concise.

prompts.prompt: explain what recursion is to someone learnin to code, use a simple everyday analogy and keep it short
prompts.rubric: Correct (a thing defined in terms of itself with a base case), one clear everyday analogy, mentions the stopping condition, beginner-friendly, concise.

prompts.prompt: write the first paragraph of a spooky short story set in an empty subway station at 3am. make it creepy but not gory
prompts.rubric: Vivid atmosphere and tension, strong sensory detail, a hook that pulls you in, restraint (creepy not gory), polished prose.

prompts.prompt: write me a short 4 line poem about autumn, something a lil wistful
prompts.rubric: Evocative autumn imagery, a wistful tone, some craft (rhythm or sound), roughly four lines, not cliche or generic.

prompts.prompt: i need a witty instagram caption for a photo from the top of a mountain i just hiked, maybe a lil pun. not too cringe
prompts.rubric: Punchy and witty, fits the summit/hiking moment, a clever (not groan-worthy) pun, appropriately short, avoids cliche caption energy.

prompts.prompt: write a balanced review of a budget phone, good battery and screen but slow and bad camera. like 100 words, be fair not ranty
prompts.rubric: Balanced pros and cons, specific not vague, a clear verdict/recommendation and who it's for, fair tone, roughly respects the length.

prompts.prompt: help me message a seller on facebook marketplace, they want 200 for a desk and i wanna offer 150 without insultin them
prompts.rubric: Friendly and respectful, makes a clear offer with a light justification, leaves room to meet in the middle, low-pressure, concise.

prompts.prompt: my friend's dog just passed away, i wanna send her a message but everything i write sounds dumb. help me say somethin that actually means something
prompts.rubric: Warm and sincere, acknowledges the specific loss (the dog), validates her grief, offers presence/support, avoids platitudes and toxic positivity.

prompts.prompt: i have to give a junior on my team feedback that their report was messy and hard to follow, but they clearly worked hard. how do i say it kindly but honestly
prompts.rubric: Specific and honest about the issues, acknowledges the effort genuinely, actionable suggestions, encouraging tone, avoids vague or harsh framing.

prompts.prompt: prove that the simple symmetric random walk in 3 dimensions is not recurrent (i.e. it is transient). keep it under 300 words
prompts.rubric: A valid proof shows the expected number of returns to the origin is finite (equivalently the return probability is < 1), typically via the local CLT estimate P(S_2n = 0) ~ c n^{-3/2}, whose sum over n converges in 3D. Hand-waving 'it spreads out so it won't come back' without the convergence argument is invalid.

prompts.prompt: given that p implies q, and that (not p) implies r, and that q is false, show that r must be true
prompts.rubric: Valid reasoning: from q false and (p -> q), modus tollens gives not p; from not p and (not p -> r), modus ponens gives r. Must actually invoke modus tollens then modus ponens, not just assert the conclusion.
